calendar.matters:
Petition For Orders To Return Trust Property, Impose Constructive Trust, Breach Of Trust And Removal, Compel An Accounting, To Instruct Trustee, And
Off Calendar Nov-13-2019 Off Calendar

Carla Ho Choys Petition For Order Approving Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Nov-19-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Graham Douds appeared by telephone for Samuel Ho (Petitioner/Beneficiary).
Kendal Fletcher for Karla Choy (Trustee).

Mr. Douds stated that he has no objections to the petition. Ms. Fletcher stated that she does not believe there are any objections and that she e-filed an amended proof of service yesterday. The Court noted that it does not think there is an issue with the 664.6 provision. Mr. Douds and Ms. Fletcher indicated that they do not think anything different. The Court granted the Petition for Order Approving Settlement Agreement (subject to review of the order). Counsel both agree that the hearing on the Petition for Orders to Return Trust Property, etc. set for December 10, 2019 can go off calendar.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Chan. Not reported.

Off Calendar Jun-18-2019 Continued To Jul-09-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Orders To Return Trust Property, Impose Constructive Trust, Breach Of Trust And Removal, Compel An Accounting, To Instruct Trustee, And
Off Calendar Mar-05-2019 Continued To Jun-18-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Graham Douds for Samuel Ho.
Kendal Fletcher for Carla Choy.

The Court stated before it is a petition seeking to return trust property (etc.), that objections have been filed, and that there are procedural issues. The Court further stated that the client's name and address needs to be listed in the petition and that the caption needs to clearly and completely identify the relief that is sought. Ms. Choy stated that she had talked to counsel, that they both want to attend mediation, that they need time to talk their respective clients, and that the matter should be continued for 120 days. Mr. Douds agreed and stated that a continuance to late June would be appropriate. At counsel's request, the Court continued the matter to June 18, 2019 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. The Court stated that it is expecting the parties to complete discovery and continue to meet and confer regarding how they wish to proceed. The Court stated that it was not sure if this matter would qualify for pro bono mediation, that the assets of the trust must be less than three million dollars, and that if counsel want to return for a referral to pro bono mediation that they can. The Court ordered a supplement to the petition to be filed that will cure the procedural issues, stated that it believed that the "answer" would need to be amended, and that a declaration would not work. The Court stated that it is a requirement that the name and address of a beneficiary be listed in the petition and that the nature of the relief sought must be clearly and completely identified.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy K. Chan. Matter Not Reported.
